ciffc.note: Off-season. National fire statistics will be updated when the 2026 fire season begins.

news.title: Wildfire funding in Sask. budget disappointing, says northern emergency co-ordinator
news.summary: Saskatchewan's wildfire preparedness funding in the provincial budget has been criticized as inadequate by a northern emergency response coordinator.

news.title: B.C. was unprepared to help Lytton rebuild after wildfire, A-G report says
news.summary: A British Columbia auditor general report reveals the province lacked adequate preparedness to support the recovery and rebuilding efforts in Lytton following the devastating 2021 wildfire.

news.title: What's the wildfire risk in Nova Scotia this year?
news.summary: Analysis of wildfire risk conditions and preparedness for Nova Scotia during the 2026 fire season.

news.title: Nova Scotia's wildfire woods ban being tested in court
news.summary: A legal challenge to Nova Scotia's wildfire prevention woods access ban has commenced in court.

news.title: Province preparing for 2026 wildfire season as Environment Canada predicts hot year ahead
news.summary: Provincial authorities are preparing for an active 2026 wildfire season following Environment Canada forecasts of hotter than normal conditions.

news.title: How far along is the Jasper wildfire rebuild?
news.summary: Update on the reconstruction and recovery progress in Jasper, Alberta following the devastating 2024 wildfire.

news.title: Village of Lytton was not prepared to lead recovery efforts following devastating fire: auditor general
news.summary: Auditor general findings show Lytton's municipal leadership was unprepared and immediately overwhelmed by wildfire recovery demands following the 2021 disaster.

news.title: PCs promised to rebuild CBN school lost to wildfire, but parents fear they're backpedaling
news.summary: Parents express concern that political commitments to rebuild a school destroyed by wildfire may not be fulfilled.

news.title: Canadian man who hiked in woods in defiance of ban has case heard in court
news.summary: A Canadian man charged with violating wildfire prevention woods access restrictions had his court hearing, highlighting tensions over fire safety regulations.

news.title: How governments can help Indigenous communities disproportionately impacted by wildfires
news.summary: Policy analysis on strategies for supporting Indigenous populations who face heightened vulnerability and disproportionate impacts from wildfire disasters.
